experiment: g-series batch (g2, g3, g4, g5, g6, g7, g8, g9) — semantics/soundness family, HS2-HS8 + HS-A
candidate: native-formalism + semantics-soundness fork family (NF1-NF7 forks of docs/design-dl-from-nsm-and-lean-reconstruction.md §7; HS-A why-now gate)
_placement_note: kot-assess/1 record at registry/assessments/<id>.json alongside f2b-replicate, compression-census, define-op-census. This is the ONE batched FABLE interpretive assessment for the whole G-series (batching per the maintainer's explicit dedup request, programme-completion loop step 1). Unlike the census assessments, TWO members (g6, g7) DO have frozen SAPs and pure-function verdict objects — those are interpreted against their verdicts. SIX members (g2, g3, g4, g5, g8, g9) have NO verdict object and NO results-log: they are NOT interpreted; they are flagged as status discrepancies against the tracker framing (see status_census + discrepancies). No interpretation here is fabricated from a design doc in place of a missing verdict.
assessor_binding: FABLE interpretive assessment per the kern Fable/Opus execution split: verdict-gen already produced the mechanical verdicts (pure function of frozen SAP + chained log); what those numbers MEAN for the correctness thesis is decided here and only here. Analyst role file .claude/agents/analyst.md; this record interprets, it does not grade, re-grade, or audit. Scope discipline: registry/assessments/ ONLY — audit-status.jsonl NOT touched (update lines reported to the coordinator for serial append); no experiment record, verdict, correction, or engine code modified; poc/gpt56-review/, poc/f2b-transfer/llmproxy*, poc/a5-llm/ untouched (live agents).

discrepancies: TRACKER-VS-REGISTRY: the tasking framing '13/14 frozen experiments run + audited, fable pending' is FALSE for six of the eight G-series rows. registry/audit-status.jsonl carries verdict_path:null for g2, g3, g4, g5, g8, g9, and tools/registry/audit-status.py renders those rows indistinguishably from run experiments awaiting assessment. Only g6 and g7 have verdicts and chained final-phase logs. The six no-verdict rows should read as NOT-RUN (four FROZEN-awaiting-inputs, two DRAFT), not as awaiting interpretation.

g4 and g5 are DRAFT — the conflicting signal the coordinator flagged is CONFIRMED: registry/experiments/g4.json and g5.json carry status DRAFT, frozen_at null. No SAP is frozen; nothing can lawfully run, and nothing is interpreted here.

g2, g3, g8, g9 are FROZEN but UNRUN: each still carries a PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder for its decisive input material (human gold set / instance descriptions / Mathlib crawl / authored explication set). Freeze != run; their audit-status notes ('audit gate fires on PASS; backfilled pre-practices') are vacuously true and misleading for unrun rows.
The codex 'n/a' state on g6/g7 is procedurally correct (the Gate-A audit gate fires on PASS; g6 is INCONCLUSIVE, g7 is FAIL), so nothing in this batch is PASS-PENDING-AUDIT.

P1's hard ordering 'G6/G7 after G4' was lawfully re-scoped pre-sign-off (corrections c-g6-1/c-g7-1, pre-signoff-reset-refreeze, before any final-phase run existed): the measured g6/g7 universe is the COMMITTED corpora, and the HS6/HS7 leg over the authored ~20-axiom working set re-registers under successor experiment ids once d-ax exists. Anyone citing g6/g7 as 'the working-set answer' is citing outside the frozen scope.

measurement_refs.scope_note: Both verdicts: rung R0 (no host model), scale language licensed: none; coverage disclosure m0b 0.3542 @ molecules-v0 (corpus-indexed, one incomplete kernel instance — NOT general coverage); extrapolation envelope verbatim P1 §4b HS2-HS8 row (kernel-size axis; re-check at 10^4-10^5 during bulk authoring; 'only corpus composition shifts them').

_surprise_note: No registered prior / claim.md exists for either deterministic count (both endpoints carry 'no statistical test, count-based fork criterion'), so no surprise-* tag is licensed. Informally: g7's margin is strongly one-sided (breach 91.3% vs the 10% bound, 9x, under accounting pinned to be biased AGAINST the kill), so the fork decision is about as unambiguous as the instrument can make it; g6's INCONCLUSIVE is the by-design outcome of a conjunctive PASS rule whose second conjunct (all-sidecar-expressible) failed while its first (share < 20%) was met by three orders of magnitude.

interpretation.headline: The G-series has produced exactly TWO mechanical results, and both are FORMALISM-CAPACITY facts, not semantics-soundness facts. g7 [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/g7.json] decisively resolves the apply-clause fork: one-level inline-only rendering breaches the 32-clause cap or 1.5x clause growth on 91.34% of 117,899 committed bulk records (bound: 10%), so kot-ast/2 apply-clauses are selected — a productive design selection, explicitly not a programme kill. g6 [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/g6.json] is INCONCLUSIVE by rule: AND-under-operator demand is rare on the committed axiom universe (142/280,910 = 0.0505%, vs the 20% bound) but only 2 of those 142 axioms are expressible in the closed kot-axiom/1 sidecar inventory, so the '∃-conjunctive-only native fragment suffices' hypothesis is neither confirmed nor killed — the grammar-capacity fork stays OPEN. Everything the correctness thesis actually stands on — Π read-out soundness (g2), the semantics pin (g3), authoring viability (g4/g9), Lean minting (g8) — is UNMEASURED: those experiments are frozen-but-unrun or DRAFT, blocked on input materials (human gold judgments, instance descriptions, a Mathlib crawl, an authored explication set), not on compute or design. The honest G-family bottom line: the native formalism's container shape is being settled cheaply and decisively; whether the semantics INSIDE the container are sound has not yet been measured at all.

g6.verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (fired rule 3, the const-true fallback; neither PASS nor FAIL rule fired)
g6.mechanical_numbers: and_share 0.0005054999822007048 over n_axioms 280,910; all_sidecar_expressible FALSE. Run-log by-source breakdown (pre-registered in the frozen design, results-log/g6.jsonl seq 0): lexical-wn31 269,960 axioms / 0 need (single-atom typed edges, zero-demand BY CONSTRUCTION, stated openly in c-g6-1); kernel-v0 and molecules-v0 sidecars 0 axioms (d-ax pending); onto-sumo 10,950 non-metadata axioms / 142 need / 2 sidecar-expressible.
g6.means_for_correctness_thesis: This is a grammar-closure question: can the native kot fragment stay ∃-conjunctive-only, with a closed sidecar inventory absorbing the residue? The measured answer on the committed corpora is split. Volume leg: AND-under-operator demand is rare — 0.0505% of the full universe, and (derived by arithmetic from the pre-registered by-source counts, all demand coming from onto-sumo because the other sources are zero-demand by construction) about 1.30% (142/10,950) of the only operator-rich committed sample — far below the 20% bound. Absorption leg: the closed sidecar inventory expresses 2/142 (~1.4%) of that demand; 140 committed SUMO-derived axioms currently have NO home — not native, not sidecar. The verdict rules were built so denominator padding cannot force a PASS (c-g6-1), and they did their job: the fragment survives on volume grounds but the 'sidecar absorbs the residue' escape hatch is MEASURED FALSE on this corpus.
g6.kill_chain_read: HS6 kill ('>=20% need + not sidecar-expressible => extend grammar') did NOT fire — no grammar extension is forced. HS6 PASS ('<20% and all sidecar-expressible => fragment stays ∃-conjunctive-only') did NOT fire either. NET: leaves the ∃-conjunctive-only sub-claim OPEN; WEAKENS (corpus-scoped) the auxiliary sub-claim that the closed kot-axiom/1 sidecar inventory is sufficient for whatever AND-demand exists. Nothing upstream or downstream in the kill chain auto-resolves off an INCONCLUSIVE.
caveats: Corpus-proxy caveat (the big one): HS6 is stated over 'the working axiom set'; what was measured is the COMMITTED corpora because the authored ~20-axiom working set (d-ax/d-axn) did not exist at freeze (lawful pre-sign-off re-scope, c-g6-1). The authored-set leg re-registers under a successor id after g4. SUMO's operator profile is a semantic-web-legacy demand SAMPLE (permitted as a corpus to count over, not a design target per directives §1) and may not resemble the NSM-native working set's profile in either direction.
The 1.30%-of-onto-sumo figure is interpretive arithmetic over pre-registered run-log counts, not a verdict-object number; the verdict-level number is and_share 0.0505% over the pinned universe.

The 140 unabsorbed axioms are unrepresentable only IN THE CURRENT closed inventory; the record does not test whether an inventory extension (vs a grammar extension) absorbs them — that choice is design work downstream of the successor leg.
R0, no host model; envelope is kernel-size-axis; re-run as regression at 10^4-10^5 during bulk authoring is pre-registered and binding.

g7.verdict: FAIL — and the frozen kill text says verbatim: 'FAIL here means the fork selects kot-ast/2 (a design selection, not a programme kill).'
g7.mechanical_numbers: breach_share 0.9133665255854587 (bound 0.10) over n_records_bulk 117,899; cap_violation_share 0.25253818946725587; growth_share 0.9130866249925784. Run-log breakdown (results-log/g7.jsonl seq 0): n_breached_either 107,685; n_cap_violations 29,774 (32-clause cap); n_growth_over_1p5x 107,652; by-source records lexical-wn31 117,791, kernel-v0 54, molecules-v0 54.
g7.means_for_correctness_thesis: Inlining relational content is not a viable bulk representation: even a ONE-LEVEL inline-only projection — pinned with both accounting choices biased AGAINST firing the kill (they understate inline pressure, c-g7-1) — breaches on 91.3% of committed bulk records, 9x over the pre-registered 10% bound. The fork therefore selects apply-clauses (kot-ast/2) as the native mechanism for relational reference. For the correctness thesis this is enabling infrastructure, not evidence of soundness: it tells you the container the axioms and explications must live in at bulk scale (reference/apply, not inline), which downstream shapes what g4 authors against, what Π (g2) reads out of, and what a Lean bridge (g8) round-trips through.
g7.kill_chain_read: KILLS the 'defer apply-clauses / inline suffices' arm of NF6 — decisively within scope. SUPPORTS adopting kot-ast/2. Does not touch any semantics sub-claim (HS2/HS3/HS8) and does not kill anything at programme level. Given the anti-kill bias of the pinned accounting, the DIRECTION is about as robust as R0 evidence gets; the exact 91.3% magnitude is composition-dependent.
caveats: Composition caveat (the big one): 117,791 of 117,899 bulk records (99.9%) are lexical-wn31 typed-edge records — reference-dense by construction. The measured 91.3% is a property of THIS committed composition; the envelope's own licensing sentence ('only corpus composition shifts them') bounds it there. The 108 native kernel/molecule records are not broken out per-source for breach in the pre-registered metrics, so no native-record breach rate is quotable.

The authored-working-set leg (P1 ordering G6/G7-after-G4) re-registers under a successor id; adopting kot-ast/2 now is licensed by the committed-bulk measurement plus the pre-registered regression re-run at 10^4-10^5 during bulk authoring.

kot-ast/2 adoption is a grammar/schema version event when implemented: encoder content-hash discipline applies (ALGORITHM_VERSION bump, X0 golden regen, Phase-X re-run) — an implementation consequence, noted here so the design selection is not treated as free.

interpretation.g2_g3_g8_g9_not_interpreted: NO interpretation is offered for g2 (Π read-out soundness vs n=500 human gold), g3 (semantics-pin necessity/sufficiency), g8 (Lean-minting fragment gate + location + round-trip), or g9 (authoring capability vs DeepNSM-8B): each is FROZEN with a sound SAP but has NO verdict and NO final-phase run — there are no numbers to interpret, and interpreting the design would be fabrication. These four are precisely the LOAD-BEARING members for the correctness thesis: g2/g3 decide whether Π is normative or demoted to lint and whether the semantics pin survives (a g3 necessity-kill auto-resolves HS2 to sidecar-only, P1 §4); g8 decides whether Lean-derived facts can be minted at all; g9 decides the why-now authoring claim. All four are Tier-0 (~$0-20) and are blocked on INPUT MATERIALS (GATE-H human gold + adjudication; instance descriptions; the d-ml Mathlib crawl; the g9.author explication set), not on compute or design. g4/g5 are DRAFT: g4 additionally depends on g2's residue list, and g5's run-condition (HS2 lands Π-as-normative) cannot even be evaluated until g2 runs.
interpretation.cross_cutting_theme: Three threads. (1) CONTAINER BEFORE CONTENT: the two decided G-results both concern the native formalism's carrying capacity, and both point the same way — committed bulk content does not fit the minimal closed forms (inline rendering breaches at 91.3%; the closed sidecar inventory absorbs ~1.4% of measured AND-demand). The formalism needs its pre-planned extension points (apply-clauses now; the grammar/inventory fork still open). This is healthy: the forks were pre-registered, decided or held open by rule, and none of it dents the theses. (2) THE SEMANTICS CORE IS UNMEASURED: no G-series evidence yet bears on whether kernel semantics are sound — the correctness thesis's G-legs are all input-blocked, and the critical path is annotator/ops material generation, not GPU or design. (3) PROXY-CORPUS DEPENDENCE: both measured verdicts are statements about semantic-web-derived committed corpora standing in for the not-yet-authored native working set; every citation must carry that scope, and the successor-id re-runs on the authored set are the pre-registered check that the proxy did not mislead.

epistemic_promotions.claim: AND-under-operator demand is rare on the committed axiom corpora (142/280,910 = 0.0505% of the pinned universe; all demand in the onto-sumo operator-rich sample)
epistemic_promotions.scope: pinned committed corpora only (kernel-v0 8209cada, lexical-wn31 5e76def8, molecules-v0 69f0c8a3, onto-sumo 80cf6eb0), pinned classifier g6_instrument.py d5cc587d; extrapolates to NO authored working set

epistemic_promotions.claim: the closed kot-axiom/1 sidecar inventory does NOT absorb the measured AND-demand (2/142 expressible)
epistemic_promotions.scope: same pins; the 140 residual axioms are SUMO-derived — whether the authored working set produces analogues is unmeasured

epistemic_promotions.claim: one-level inline-only rendering breaches cap-or-1.5x-growth on >10% of committed bulk records (measured 91.34%, n=117,899), so apply-clauses (kot-ast/2) are selected
epistemic_promotions.scope: committed bulk universe, 99.9% lexical-wn31 records; one-level inline projection pinned biased against the kill; regression re-run pre-registered at 10^4-10^5 during bulk authoring

epistemic_promotions.claim: HS6 as stated (∃-conjunctive-only fragment suffices) — NO promotion in either direction (verdict INCONCLUSIVE); any statement about the authored working set's operator demand, native-record breach rates, Π soundness, pin validity, Lean minting, or authoring capability
epistemic_promotions.scope: no promotion; g2/g3/g8/g9 have no verdicts and g4/g5 no freeze

forks_resolved.resolution: RESOLVED -> kot-ast/2 apply-clauses, by fired pre-registered rule (breach_share 0.9134 > 0.10). Design selection, not a programme kill (verbatim kill text). Scope: committed bulk corpora; authored-set successor leg + bulk-authoring regression re-run remain pre-registered checks.

OPEN — INCONCLUSIVE by rule. Demand-volume leg met by three orders of magnitude; absorption leg measured false (2/142). Neither grammar extension forced nor fragment-closure confirmed. Re-decided at the authored-set successor leg (post-g4) and the bulk-authoring regression.

forks_resolved.fork: NF1/HS2 (Π read-out), NF7/HS3 (semantics pin), NF2/HS4 (authoring surface), NF4/HS5 (constraints out of vector), NF3/HS8 (Lean minting), HS-A (why-now)
forks_resolved.resolution: UNRESOLVED — no runs exist. Not interpreted here (no fabrication).

mechanism_state: g7: resolved (kot-ast/2 selected); g6: open (re-entry = authored-set successor leg post-g4, plus the 10^4-10^5 bulk-authoring regression); g2/g3/g8/g9: frozen-awaiting-inputs; g4/g5: draft

questions_opened.question: The correctness thesis's G-critical-path is INPUT MATERIALS, not compute: g2 needs the Π projector (d-pi) + n=500 blind-adjudicated gold (GATE-H ~12h annotator ops); g3 needs ~200 instance-description judgments; g8 needs the d-ml 1,000-declaration Mathlib crawl; g9 needs the N>=50 authored explication set + validator outputs. Sequencing note from P1: g3 is upstream of g2's interpretation (a g3 necessity-kill auto-resolves HS2 to sidecar-only) — generating g3 materials first is the cheapest way to avoid wasting the g2 gold-annotation budget.

Pre-registered by c-g6-1: once d-ax exists (post-g4), re-register the HS6 count over the authored working axiom set under a successor experiment id (preserving the P1 G4-before-G6 ordering for that leg). This is the measurement that decides whether the 140-axiom sidecar-absorption gap matters for the programme's OWN content or is a SUMO artifact.

kot-ast/2 adoption is now licensed within scope; implementing it is an encoder/grammar version event (ALGORITHM_VERSION bump, X0 golden regen, Phase-X re-run) and should be scheduled deliberately, not slipped in. The pre-registered 10^4-10^5 bulk-authoring regression re-run of g6/g7 doubles as its acceptance check.

Make tools/registry/audit-status.py distinguish NOT-RUN (verdict_path null) from run-awaiting-assessment, so the tracker cannot again present six unrun experiments as a 13/14-complete interpretation backlog.

_questions_note: est_cost_usd / est_agent_days are STIPULATED dry-plan estimates, never measurements. The g-series-unblock-materials estimate inherits P1 Tier-0 caps (~$0-20 + annotator hours).

ledger_updates.statement: Apply-clauses (kot-ast/2) selected: one-level inline-only rendering breaches the 32-clause cap or >1.5x clause growth on 91.34% of 117,899 committed bulk records (cap violations 25.25%, growth 91.31%), 9x over the pre-registered 10% bound, under accounting pinned biased AGAINST the kill. Design selection, not a programme kill.
ledger_updates.scope: g7, R0; committed bulk universe (99.9% lexical-wn31 records); one-level inline projection; envelope: re-check at 10^4-10^5 during bulk authoring

ledger_updates.statement: ∃-conjunctive-only fragment: AND-under-operator demand rare (142/280,910 = 0.0505% of the committed universe; all in onto-sumo) but only 2/142 sidecar-expressible — the closed kot-axiom/1 inventory does not absorb the residue. INCONCLUSIVE by rule; grammar-capacity fork stays open pending the authored-set successor leg.
ledger_updates.scope: g6, R0; pinned committed corpora; NOT the authored working set

ledger_updates.statement: Six of eight G-series experiments have NO verdict: g2/g3/g8/g9 FROZEN-awaiting-input-materials, g4/g5 DRAFT. The correctness thesis's semantics core (Π soundness, pin validity, Lean minting, authoring capability) is entirely UNMEASURED as of this assessment; its critical path is annotator/ops material generation.

assumptions_resolved.id: g-series/assumption: committed corpora are an adequate demand proxy for the authored working set (STIPULATED, c-g6-1/c-g7-1)
assumptions_resolved.backing_ref: Both g6/g7 verdicts are scoped to the committed corpora; the authored-set successor legs are the pre-registered resolution path. Re-registered still-open; no conclusion in this assessment treats the proxy as the working set.

assumptions_resolved.id: g-series/assumption: formal-fragment properties don't vary with observer scale, only corpus composition shifts them (envelope licensing assumption, P1 §4b)
assumptions_resolved.backing_ref: Used only as the frozen envelope's own licensing sentence; the 10^4-10^5 bulk-authoring regression re-run is its check. Never used here to widen a number beyond its pinned corpus.
m0b verdict; restated verbatim in both g6/g7 verdicts' coverage disclosures; NOT general coverage, extrapolates to no other corpus/rung/kernel state.
g2 unrun; the condition is undetermined, so g5's DRAFT state is design-consistent, not merely backlog.

boundary_stops.item: Generating the missing input materials (g2 gold set + adjudication protocol execution, g3 instance descriptions, d-ml Mathlib crawl, g9 authored explication set) and freezing g4/g5
boundary_stops.reason: Ops + design work outside the analyst role and outside this assessment's scope; g2/g3 additionally involve human-annotation (GATE-H) decisions that are the maintainer's. Interpretive input: these four are the correctness thesis's load-bearing measurements and all are Tier-0 cheap; g3-before-g2 ordering protects the gold-annotation budget.

boundary_stops.item: Appending the fable_interpretive_assessed updates to registry/audit-status.jsonl
boundary_stops.reason: Shared append-only file under central custody; exact lines reported to the coordinator for serial append, per tasking.

tree_impact: G-family tree after this assessment: g7 leaf CLOSED (kot-ast/2 selected; implementation gate + bulk-authoring regression pre-registered as follow-ups). g6 leaf remains OPEN with a defined re-entry (authored-set successor id post-g4 + bulk regression); its one hard new fact is that the closed sidecar inventory absorbs ~none (2/142) of measured AND-demand. g2/g3/g8/g9 are the live critical path for the correctness thesis and are materials-blocked, not compute-blocked; g4/g5 stay DRAFT pending g2/g3 outcomes by design. Fable interpretive assessment is DONE for g6 and g7 ONLY; the other six G-ids cannot be assessed until verdicts exist, and marking them 'done' would launder unrun experiments — the coordinator should instead correct their tracker rows to a not-run state.
